DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT JENNY JIMENEZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, as to the first, second, third and fifth causes of action is OVERRULED as to the first, second and third causes of action and SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. Liberally construing the second amended cross-complaint, Ms. Jimenez is sued on the first three causes of action, among other things, as the alter ego of Kingston Wellness. Because those causes of action are not time-barred as to Kingston Wellness, they are not time-barred as to Ms. Jimenez as the alleged alter ego of Kingston Wellness. (Hennessey's Tavern, Inc. v. American Filter Company, Inc. (1988) 204 Cal. App. 3d 1351, 1355). Because a demurrer is sustained to a cause of action only when the entire cause of action fails on every theory pled, Ms. Jimenez' demurrer to the first three causes of action must be overruled. Since the fifth cause of action for conversion is alleged only against Ms. Jimenez, it appears that she is being sued on that cause of action only for her own alleged misconduct, not as the alter ego of Kingston Wellness, and thus that cause of action is time-barred since Ms. Angeles' cross-complaint was filed more than three years after she discovered the alleged conversion by Ms. Jimenez. Cross-complainant Monica Angeles' argument that Ms. Jimenez is an indispensable party lacks merit. Even if she is an indispensable party, which she is not, the indispensable party rule does not allow a party to sue on a claim that is time-barred. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Attorney, Lauren Vasquez, Esq. (805-217-6116) appears as counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant/demurring party, Kingston Wellness, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT KINGSTON WELLNESS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT as to strike the third, fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action is DENIED. Moreover, the policy of the law is to liberally allow amendments of pleadings. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hasmik Geghamyan of Geghamyah Law Office telephone number 818-590-0070 for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Monica Angeles.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT KINGSTON WELLNESS INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is granted as to the punitive damages allegations and denied as to all other allegations sought to be stricken. Cross-Complainant Monica Angeles acknowledges that her punitive damages allegations should be stricken. A motion to strike does not lie as to the entirety of a cause of action, but even if it did, there is no basis to strike the entirety of the third cause of action because of the assertedly false statements contained therein since the third cause of action is not dependent on those statements. Nor is there any basis to strike the words "temporary" or "director" from the cross-complaint. Those words in the cross-complaint do not prejudice Kingston Wellness. The prevailing paty is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hasmik Geghamyan of Geghamyah Law Office telephone number 818-590-0070 for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Monica Angeles.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Ms. Geghamyan explained that her Client is out of town and requested more time to amend. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling with one modification as to the number of days to amend. CROSS-DEFENDANT KINGSTON WELLNESS INC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to all three claims alleged in the first amended cross-complaint. Cross-Complainant Monica Angeles has not pled facts showing that the condition which requiring Kingston Wellness to repay the loan has occurred and is granted leave to correct this deficiency if she can do so in good faith. All other arguments made in support of the demurrer lack merit. The face of the first amended complaint does not necessarily show that any of the claims are time-barred, particularly given that the relation back doctrine applies to the filing of a cross-complaint. Nor does the face of the first amended complaint or the matters that are sought to be judicially noticed necessarily show that any of the claims are based on an illegal contract or that, even if they were, the contract cannot be enforced for equitable reasons. Nor do any of the claims fail because of insufficient allegations regarding communication of or acceptance of an offer. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
